scope.summary: Implement edge-split operation on Panel EdgeShape outlines (subdivide a Line or Bezier edge at a parametric t-value, inserting a new vertex into the GarmentSpec panel outline)
acceptance_criteria: split_edge(panel_id, edge_index, t: f32) splits the edge at parameter t, producing two edges that share the new vertex; the panel outline remains closed and PANEL_CLOSED passes
Split is CRDT-safe: emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded with the diff; concurrent splits on different edges merge without conflict
The new vertex coordinates in cm are stored in tailor_panels spec_json
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=split_edge
Bezier edge split follows de Casteljau algorithm preserving the original curve shape within eps_mm=0.1
scope.risk_if_missed: Without edge subdivision, panel outlines can only be shaped at construction time. Adding a dart apex, notch, or seam attachment point to an existing edge is impossible — a core MD workflow step (Add Point) is blocked. All downstream dart, pleat, and seam-length-match operations that depend on split points fail.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
